summary: The fake training session tested an approval-gated AI workflow for meeting follow-up.
The MVP keeps Google Sheets as the review layer and sends a short Telegram summary only after approval.

action_items.notes: Include accuracy, privacy, and missing-owner checks.

action_items.task: Collect common employee questions from the next two demo sessions.
action_items.notes: Use the results to expand the FAQ section for future runs.

key_decisions: Use Google Sheets as the MVP review layer.
Keep the portfolio workflow limited to fake/demo transcripts.
risks_open_questions: Reviewers may treat AI output as approved if the human review step is not visible enough.
The long-term production review layer may need something stronger than Google Sheets.

employee_faq.question: Can real customer data be pasted into the transcript box?
employee_faq.answer: No. The portfolio demo must use fake/demo data only.

ai_adoption_opportunities.title: Create a repeatable follow-up package after each training session.
ai_adoption_opportunities.notes: Reduces repeated manual drafting and creates a standard reviewer surface.

ai_ambassador_brief.headline: AI drafts the follow-up, but humans approve the message.
talking_points: The workflow extracts structured review data from a transcript.
A reviewer can approve, edit, or reject the delivery draft.
Telegram runs only after explicit approval.
ai_ambassador_brief.suggested_internal_message: We tested a fake-data workflow that drafts follow-up content from transcripts, routes it through review, and sends a short internal summary only after approval.
